conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm inquiring about my child. I have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 6 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you some questions:

**Are you responsible for Alex? That is, do you live with Alex and are you responsible for their care (such as providing for their food, clothing, and general welfare)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't live with me. They live elsewhere.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to understand the situation better.

**Who does Alex currently live with, and do you contribute to their upbringing in any way (for example, do you pay towards their care or support them financially)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with their other parent. I'm not currently contributing financially to their upbringing.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. 

Just to make sure I have a complete picture of the situation:

**Are you the person who is mainly responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, or is the other parent the one who is primarily responsible for looking after Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: The other parent is the one who is primarily responsible for looking after Alex on a day-to-day basis.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Based on the information you've provided, I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Result: You are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

**Here's why:**

To qualify for Child Benefit, you must be "responsible" for the child. This means:
- You need to live with the child, AND
- You need to be responsible for their care (providing for their food, clothing, and general welfare)

Since Alex lives with their other parent and that parent is primarily responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, you do not meet the responsibility requirement. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child at a time, and this is typically the person the child lives with who provides their care.

In this case, Alex's other parent (the person Alex lives with) would be the eligible person to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: To be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, you must be responsible for the child. This means you need to live with the child and be responsible for their care (providing for their food, clothing, and general welfare). Since Alex does not live with you, and the other parent is primarily responsible for Alex's day-to-day care, you do not meet the responsibility requirement. Only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and this would typically be the person the child lives with who is responsible for their care - in this case, Alex's other parent.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 6), who lives with their other parent. The claimant does not live with Alex, does not contribute financially, and is not primarily responsible for Alex's day-to-day care. Under Child Benefit rules, you must be responsible for a child (live with them and provide for their care) to be eligible. The other parent, who Alex lives with and who provides day-to-day care, would be the eligible person to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
